examples.title: Page Fault Frequency Control Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to monitor and control thrashing using page fault frequency
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PFF_Control
BEGIN
    SET upper_threshold = 0.7  // 70% page fault rate
    SET lower_threshold = 0.1  // 10% page fault rate
    
    FOR each process P IN ready_queue DO
        SET faults = CountPageFaults(P, time_interval)
        SET accesses = CountMemoryAccesses(P, time_interval)
        SET fault_rate = faults / accesses
        
        IF fault_rate > upper_threshold THEN
            // Process thrashing - allocate more frames
            AllocateFrames(P, additional_frames)
        ELSE IF fault_rate < lower_threshold THEN
            // Process not utilizing frames efficiently
            ReclaimFrames(P, reclaim_count)
        END IF
    END FOR
    
    IF system_wide_fault_rate > critical_limit THEN
        // System-wide thrashing - suspend a process
        P = SelectProcessToSuspend()
        SuspendProcess(P)
    END IF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm monitors each process's page fault rate over a time interval. If the rate exceeds the upper threshold, more frames are allocated to prevent thrashing. If below the lower threshold, frames are reclaimed for other processes. If the entire system is thrashing, a process is suspended.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of processes

examples.title: Working Set Calculation
examples.description: Python code to calculate working set and detect potential thrashing
examples.code:
def calculate_working_set(reference_string, window_size):
    """
    Calculate the working set for a given reference string and window size.
    """
    working_sets = []
    
    for i in range(len(reference_string)):
        start = max(0, i - window_size + 1)
        window = reference_string[start:i+1]
        ws = set(window)  # Unique pages in window
        working_sets.append(ws)
    
    return working_sets

def check_thrashing(working_sets, allocated_frames):
    """
    Check if working set size exceeds allocated frames (thrashing condition).
    """
    for i, ws in enumerate(working_sets):
        if len(ws) > allocated_frames:
            print(f"Time {i}: THRASHING - Working set {len(ws)} > Frames {allocated_frames}")
            return True
    return False

# Example usage
reference_string = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
window_size = 4
allocated_frames = 3

ws = calculate_working_set(reference_string, window_size)
print("Working sets:", ws)
print("Thrashing detected:", check_thrashing(ws, allocated_frames))
examples.explanation: This code simulates working set calculation. It tracks which pages have been referenced within a sliding window (representing recent time). If the working set size (unique pages in window) exceeds the allocated frames, thrashing will occur.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m*n) where m=reference length, n=window size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w=window size for set storage

examples.title: Thrashing Detection Simulation
examples.description: C program to simulate thrashing detection using page fault counting
examples.explanation: This C program simulates a basic paging system with FIFO replacement. It tracks page faults and calculates the fault rate. If the fault rate exceeds 50%, it warns about thrashing. The simulation demonstrates how insufficient frames lead to more page faults.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n*f) where n=references, f=number of frames

examples.title: Process Suspension Controller
examples.description: Java code to manage process suspension when thrashing is detected
examples.explanation: This Java implementation demonstrates a thrashing control system using process suspension. When a process exceeds the high fault rate threshold (70%), it either gets more frames or another process is suspended to free frames. The lowest priority process is selected for suspension to minimize impact.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) for finding lowest priority process
